temperature.note: Stable, within range. 24hr trend +1.8°F slight climb but still comfortable.

ph.note: Slightly acidic, within range. +0.04 drift—continues slow acidification trend from bioload.

tds.note: Healthy accumulation mid-range. +6.0ppm reflects nitrate buildup (80ppm confirms).

reasoning: Nitrogen cycle progressing on schedule: ammonia cleared to 0 ppm (spike resolved), nitrite plateaued at 2.0 ppm for 3 days suggesting Nitrobacter population lag rather than stall, nitrate robust at 80 ppm. All physical parameters green and stable; pH acidification is normal for active bioload but must monitor for pH collapse below 6.3 in next 48 hours.
recommended_actions: Continue daily observation of ammonia and nitrite levels—watch for decline in nitrite or recurrent ammonia spikes.
Monitor pH trend closely over next 48 hours; if drops below 6.3, consider water change.
No action needed now; cycle is responding as expected for day 16.
suggested_actuator_actions: None—system stable. Hold heater and aeration at current settings.

temperature.note: Within target range; gentle upward drift over 24h (+1.58°F). Stable and healthy.

ph.note: Center of target range; slight acidification drift is normal in cycling tank. Monitor for further drops.

tds.note: Well within safe range; minor 24h rise likely from bioload accumulation and nitrate production.

reasoning: Ammonia cleared to zero despite recent spike—fast bacterial response suggests robust nitrification starting. Nitrite plateau at 2.0ppm indicates Nitrobacter colonization lag, not cycle failure; this is textbook mid-cycle behavior on day 16.
recommended_actions: Continue daily observation of ammonia and nitrite trends
Flag immediately if pH drops below 6.3 or ammonia reappears above 0.5ppm
Expect nitrite decline within 3–5 days as Nitrobacter population ramps
suggested_actuator_actions: None at this time—tank parameters are stable and cycle is progressing normally

temperature.note: Stable, within target. 24hr mean 77.3°F, trending warm (+1.57°F). Monitor for continued rise.

ph.note: Acceptable. Slight upward drift (+0.08 in 24h). Below target midpoint but safe. Watch if drops below 6.3.

tds.note: Healthy. Within target. Rising gradually (+3.0ppm/24h) from bioload accumulation—expected during cycle.

reasoning: Ammonia crash to zero paired with zero nitrite spike suggests Nitrobacter (stage-2 bacteria) is now active and consuming nitrite, though plateau at 2.0ppm for 3 days indicates population is still building strength. Temperature creep is mild and temperature remains well-buffered; pH is stable despite slight acidification from bioload.
recommended_actions: Continue daily observation—no intervention needed yet.
Log next water test in 48 hours; if ammonia reappears >0.25ppm, cycle may be stalling.
If pH drops below 6.3 in next 48 hours, consider small (10–15%) water change with fresh RO/distilled water.
Track nitrite for any decline—even gradual decrease would be positive milestone.
suggested_actuator_actions: None at this time. Heating/cooling stable. Aeration adequate.
When available: set heater to maintain 76–77°F (current trajectory is healthy).
